name: architect-editor-pair

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready
Instantiation (b): plan(architect)->implement(editor) mode-pair, one model. Distinct from user-blocking-plan-mode-approval (2.18), which stops at the waiting gate; this captures the post-approval editor phase + cross-phase token accumulation.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

(Reassessment under the broadened architect-editor-pair meaning. The prior 2026-05-23 verdict `no` was correct ONLY for the narrow Aider-shaped reading — two distinct models within one turn — which Claude Code cannot produce. The scenario meaning now admits a second instantiation, and Claude Code satisfies it.)

## Broadened scenario: two instantiations

The architect/editor essence is `a planning/architect contribution produces a plan, then an editing/editor contribution applies the edits`. Two instantiations (scenario-meanings.md#architect-editor-pair): (a) two MODELS in one turn (Aider --architect); (b) a plan->implement MODE pair across the approval gate (Claude Code plan mode). This cell is instantiation (b).

## How Claude Code instantiates it (agent_supports: yes)

- **Architect = plan phase.** Claude Code's plan mode (entered via `permissions.defaultMode=plan`, the same primitive user-blocking-plan-mode-approval (2.18) uses) constrains the agent to analyze and PROPOSE a plan. When the plan is ready the agent emits an `ExitPlanMode` tool_use; the TUI shows an approve/reject overlay and the agent pauses. This is the planning/architect contribution.
- **Editor = implement phase.** When the user APPROVES (selecting "auto-accept edits" in the overlay), the agent exits plan mode and applies the edits (real Edit/Write), then the turn completes. This is the editing/editor contribution.
- One model is used throughout (Claude Code captures one ModelName per requestId); this is the mode-pair instantiation, not the Aider two-model shape, which is exactly what the broadened meaning allows.

## irrlicht observability (daemon_capability: full)

The full arc `ready -> working -> waiting -> working -> ready` is produced by shipped code — the same machinery 2.18 validates for the ENTRY, extended through the approval continuation:

1. **working (architect/plan phase).** The user prompt drives `ready -> working` (force-on-first-activity).
2. **waiting (plan-approval gate).** `ExitPlanMode` is in the user-blocking tool set (core/pkg/tailer/tailer_config.go); the PreToolUse hook matches `toolExitPlanMode` (core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/hooks.go) and sets PermissionPending, so ClassifyState routes `working -> waiting` while the open tool keeps `HasOpenToolCall=true` / `IsAgentDone()=false` (no intervening ready).
3. **working (editor/implement phase).** On approval the tool_result lands, `HasOpenToolCall` clears, and the next assistant message drives `waiting -> working` (state_classifier.go recovery; 2.18's own assessment documents this Variant-1 continuation).
4. **ready.** The eventual turn_done lands `working -> ready`.
5. **Token accumulation + ModelName.** The claudecode parser accumulates per-turn token contributions into SessionMetrics.TotalTokens across both phases, and ModelName stays a single consistent value (one model). The editor phase carries a real Edit/Write tool_result.

2.18 asserts ONLY items 1-2 (it terminates in waiting). This cell asserts the full 1-5, i.e. the post-approval editor phase + cross-phase token growth + the real edit. That is the user-observable distinction (not an internal flag), so the two cells do not collapse.

## driver_capability: ready

The claudecode interactive driver implements send / sleep / wait_turn / keys (and slash/interrupt). The recipe needs only: settings `defaultMode=plan` (+ pre-allow Edit/Write so the editor phase does not hit a SECOND permission gate), a `send` (the task), a `sleep` to reach the waiting gate, a `keys` to APPROVE the ExitPlanMode overlay (exact keystroke discovered at record time, as with the model-switch /model cache-confirm dialog), and `wait_turn` for the implement phase. No absent step type -> driver ready, not a gap.

## Distinctness (must hold)

vs user-blocking-plan-mode-approval (2.18): same plan->ExitPlanMode->waiting entry, but 2.18 has `final_state: waiting` and captures ONLY the gate; this cell continues to `final_state: ready` through the editor/implement phase. vs model-switch-midsession (5.3): that is a user-driven cross-turn model change (two ModelNames); this is one model across a plan/implement mode pair.
caveats: This is the MODE-PAIR instantiation (one model: plan phase = architect, implement phase = editor), per the broadened architect-editor-pair meaning. It is NOT the Aider two-models-in-one-turn shape; Claude Code captures one ModelName per requestId.

Distinct from user-blocking-plan-mode-approval (2.18): 2.18 terminates at the waiting gate (final_state waiting); this cell continues past approval through the editor/implement phase to ready, asserting the post-approval waiting->working->ready continuation + a real edit + cross-phase token accumulation.
The recipe MUST avoid a second permission gate in the editor phase (pre-allow Edit/Write via settings and/or approve with 'auto-accept edits') — a second waiting episode would muddy the arc.

The ExitPlanMode approval is driven via a `keys` step; the exact overlay keystroke is discovered live at record time (cf. model-switch-midsession's /model cache-confirm dialog handled with keys 1 + Enter).

sources.note: ExitPlanMode is in the user-blocking tool set — drives working->waiting at the plan-approval gate (architect phase end).
PreToolUse handler matches toolExitPlanMode and flips PermissionPending -> waiting (same path 2.18 uses).
waiting->working->ready recovery after approval (2.18 Variant-1 continuation) — the editor/implement phase this cell additionally captures.
per-turn ModelName + token contribution accumulation into SessionMetrics across both phases (one consistent model).
architect-editor-pair broadened to admit instantiation (b): plan(architect)->implement(editor) mode-pair; this cell instantiates it.
sibling 2.18 cell — same plan-mode entry, but final_state waiting; this cell is the post-approval continuation (distinctness anchor).

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
claude --settings honours permissions.defaultMode=plan AND permissions.allow (verified in 2.1.x)
irrlichd is recording — this cell is recorded in --attach mode against the user's running daemon (claudecode hooks POST to a hardcoded :7837, so coexist/isolated-alt-port modes don't apply)
no other claudecode session uses the same UUID
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID.
Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude prompts for trust on first encounter — the driver auto-accepts).

The settings blob engages plan mode (permissions.defaultMode=plan, the same primitive 2.18 uses for the architect/plan phase) AND pre-allows Edit+Write (permissions.allow) so the editor/implement phase applies the edits WITHOUT hitting a SECOND permission gate — a second waiting episode would muddy the full arc this cell asserts.

The ExitPlanMode approval is the load-bearing continuation past 2.18: after the agent emits ExitPlanMode the TUI shows an approve overlay (1. Yes, and auto-accept edits / 2. Yes, manually approve / 3. No, keep planning). The recipe approves by pressing `1` (auto-accept edits) then Enter — choosing auto-accept means the implement phase runs ungated even beyond the pre-allowed Edit/Write. The exact keystroke was settled live at record time (cf. model-switch-midsession's /model cache-confirm dialog, handled with keys 1 + Enter).
Belt-and-suspenders: BOTH the auto-accept overlay option AND permissions.allow Edit/Write guard against a second waiting gate in the implement phase.

script.text: You are in plan mode. ExitPlanMode is how you PROPOSE a plan BEFORE doing any work — you do NOT need to (and must not) read, write, explore, or search anything first. Call ExitPlanMode immediately as your first and only tool call. Pass this exact plan in the `plan` field: 'Create calc.py with two functions: add(a, b) returning a + b, and sub(a, b) returning a - b.' After I approve, create the file calc.py with exactly those two functions and nothing else — do not explore or search, just write the file.

verify: events.jsonl shows the session appear in ready within ~2s of launch (plan mode engaged via settings).
events.jsonl shows a state_transition to working on the session when the task prompt lands (architect/plan phase).
events.jsonl shows a state_transition to waiting on the SAME session once the agent emits the ExitPlanMode tool_use (plan-approval gate), with NO state_transition to ready before the user approves.
events.jsonl shows a state_transition to working on the SAME session after the user approves (keys 1 + Enter on the ExitPlanMode overlay) — the editor/implement phase recovers waiting→working.
events.jsonl shows a state_transition to ready on the SAME session when the implement turn finishes (working→ready).

transcript.jsonl contains an assistant message with an ExitPlanMode tool_use (architect/plan phase) AND, after approval, a real Edit or Write tool_use + tool_result that creates calc.py (editor/implement phase) — a genuine editor contribution, not a stop-at-waiting.
the file calc.py is actually created in the cwd with add(a,b) and sub(a,b).
SessionMetrics.TotalTokens after turn_end > at plan_gate (tokens accumulate across the plan and implement phases) — asserted in prose; the events.jsonl phase DSL has no token field.
ModelName is a single consistent value across both phases (one model — this is the mode-pair instantiation, NOT the Aider two-model shape).
recipe.notes: Instantiation (b) of architect-editor-pair: Claude Code's plan→implement MODE pair (one model) captured across the approval gate — the FULL arc ready→working→waiting→working→ready. This is the post-approval CONTINUATION of user-blocking-plan-mode-approval (2.18): 2.18 records the SAME plan→ExitPlanMode→waiting entry but TERMINATES at the waiting gate (final_state waiting); this cell continues PAST approval through the editor/implement phase to ready, asserting the waiting→working→ready recovery + a real Edit/Write + cross-phase token accumulation. Stopping at waiting here would be a duplicate of 2.18 = FAILURE. ARCHITECT (plan phase): plan mode is engaged via the settings blob permissions.defaultMode=plan (the same primitive 2.18 uses); the prompt (borrowed verbatim from 2.18's proven phrasing) instructs the agent to call ExitPlanMode IMMEDIATELY as its first and only tool call — no read/write/explore/search first — passing the calc.py plan in the `plan` field. This determinism matters: a looser prompt let the agent run a ToolSearch + plans-Write BEFORE ExitPlanMode on one run, so the overlay wasn't up at the fixed approve-sleep mark and the keystrokes missed it (wait_turn timed out). Forbidding exploration makes ExitPlanMode reliably the first action so the overlay is up well before the 30s approve sleep. The ExitPlanMode tool_use gates the session into waiting. EDITOR (implement phase): the recipe APPROVES the ExitPlanMode overlay with `keys 1` + `keys Enter` — option 1 is 'Yes, and auto-accept edits', so the implement phase runs UNGATED (no second permission prompt → no spurious second waiting episode). Belt-and-suspenders: the settings blob ALSO pre-allows Edit+Write (permissions.allow) so the editor phase is ungated even independent of the auto-accept choice. After approval the agent applies a real Write/Edit creating calc.py and the turn completes →ready (wait_turn + a 20s trailing sleep let the working→ready settle land before the daemon stops observing — a first record with only 6s tore the process down ~2s after the final end_turn, before the classifier emitted working→ready, so the recording ended in waiting; the 20s trailing sleep fixes that race). APPROVAL KEYSTROKE: settled live at record time as `1` then `Enter` (the overlay's option 1 = auto-accept edits), mirroring how model-switch-midsession answered claude's /model cache-confirm dialog with keys 1 + Enter. WHY a wait_turn for the implement turn (not the plan turn): the plan turn ends in waiting (not a completed turn), so a wait_turn there would block forever; the plan-gate is reached via a fixed 45s sleep instead, and wait_turn covers ONLY the post-approval implement turn which DOES complete. What differs between runs: token counts/cost, UUID/PID/timestamps, the exact plan wording, and the alias→version ModelName mapping (one consistent value, assert presence + single-ness, not a hardcoded string). What must NOT differ: the full ready→working→waiting→working→ready arc on ONE session, a real Edit/Write creating calc.py in the implement phase, no SECOND waiting episode (permission gate) in the implement phase, and TotalTokens growing from plan_gate to turn_end. RECORDED in --attach mode against the dev irrlichd --record on :7837 (claudecode hooks hardcode :7837, so no coexist/isolated-alt-port).

description: Claude Code's plan→implement MODE pair (one model) captured across the approval gate — the FULL lifecycle arc ready→working→waiting→working→ready. Instantiation (b) of architect-editor-pair: the architect is the PLAN phase (plan mode via permissions.defaultMode=plan; the agent proposes a plan via ExitPlanMode → waiting), the editor is the IMPLEMENT phase (the user approves → waiting→working; the agent applies a real Edit/Write and the turn completes → ready). Distinct from user-blocking-plan-mode-approval (2.18), which terminates at the waiting gate; this cell continues PAST approval through the editor/implement phase, asserting the post-approval continuation + a real edit + cross-phase token accumulation. One model throughout (mode-pair, not the Aider two-model shape).
